full_prompt: Design the four landing legs of a LASC 2026 Lander class payload that is ejected from the nose cone of a 3000 m apogee sounding rocket at apogee, descends under a parachute to approximately 30 m AGL, then uses four cold-gas thrusters to decelerate and land softly on a flat surface at a target vertical velocity of 2 m/s. Lander total dry mass 3.5 kg; with cold-gas propellant and electronics total wet mass at landing 4.2 kg. Envelope: 250 mm diameter body cylinder + 4 deployable legs on a 400 mm pitch circle when extended. Each leg is a CFRP pultruded rod of 20 mm OD x 2 mm wall, length 220 mm, angled 30 degrees outward from vertical, terminating in a 50 mm rubber landing foot. Material: CFRP UD (E 140 GPa, tensile 1600 MPa, compressive 1100 MPa, density 1550 kg/m^3). Legs fold up into the body during ascent and deploy via spring-release on cold-gas activation. Load cases: (LC1) ascent stowed: 20 g axial on folded legs (launch-qualification equivalent on Lander; sees full rocket ascent acceleration); (LC2) leg deployment kickout: spring produces 80 N tension at leg-to-body hinge during 100 ms deploy; (LC3) touchdown at 2 m/s on flat ground: 4.2 kg lander decelerates over 20 mm stroke of rubber foot; energy = 0.5 x 4.2 x 2^2 = 8.4 J over 20 mm = equivalent average 420 N per leg vertical, peak assumed 1.5x average = 630 N per leg; (LC4) off-axis touchdown: 2 of 4 legs contact first with 2x load on those two legs = 1260 N each; (LC5) wind-tip test: 50 N horizontal applied at body CoM while lander stands on legs on ground - verify stability margin by computing moment about leg-to-ground contact circle. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1-LC4, max CFRP leg stress must remain below 800 MPa (1600 / 2.0 FoS) tensile, 550 MPa (1100 / 2.0) compressive; (R2) leg-hinge shear pin (3 mm 316 stainless, yield 215 MPa) must remain below 107 MPa under LC2 deployment; (R3) leg-tip deflection under LC4 off-axis touchdown must remain below 8 mm to preserve stable geometry; (R4) lander must not tip over under LC5 (quasi-static stability check); (R5) combined 4-leg dry mass must not exceed 180 g; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue on a single leg under LC4 must exceed 2.0. Thermal, fluid, radiation analyses not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the 4 legs + 4 hinges + 4 feet (as separate bodies), FEA per case, buckling for LC4, stability calculation for LC5, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the four landing legs of a LASC 2026 Lander-class payload. Ejected from the nose cone of a 3000 m apogee sounding rocket at apogee, descends under parachute to ~30 m AGL, then four cold-gas thrusters decelerate to a 2 m/s vertical-velocity target landing. Lander dry 3.5 kg; landing wet 4.2 kg; 250 mm OD body cylinder; legs deploy on a 400 mm pitch circle.

Envelope: each leg is a 20 mm OD x 2 mm wall x 220 mm pultruded UD CFRP rod angled 30 deg outward, 50 mm rubber foot, folded during ascent and spring-released on cold-gas activation.

Factor regime: CFRP FoS 2.0 (tensile 800 MPa / compressive 550 MPa); 316 SST hinge-pin yield FoS 2.0 (107 MPa); leg-tip deflection under off-axis touchdown < 8 mm (stability); wind-tip stability is a moment-about-contact-circle check (no stress gate); combined 4-leg dry mass <= 180 g; linear buckling eigenvalue on a single leg under LC4 >= 2.0.

Load cases.
- Ascent stowed: 20 g axial on the folded legs (launch qualification).
- Leg deployment kickout: 80 N spring tension at the leg-to-body hinge over 100 ms.
- Touchdown at 2 m/s: 8.4 J over a 20 mm foot stroke = 420 N avg per leg, 630 N peak (1.5x avg).
- Off-axis touchdown: 2 of 4 legs contact first = 1260 N each.
- Wind-tip stability: 50 N horizontal at CoM with lander standing on legs.

prompt.title: LASC 2026 Lander Class 4-Leg Landing Gear
prompt.brief: 4 CFRP legs for 4.2 kg wet lander, 2 m/s vertical touchdown, 3000 m deploy apogee, LASC lander scheme.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (BEAM188) for legs; shell for foot pad; solid for hinge pin

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: LASC 2026 official Lander-class rulebook not yet published as stand-alone PDF; catalog cites 2026 overview page and 2023 rules mirror. Confirm touchdown-velocity spec, envelope, and wet-mass cap when the PDF releases.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Parachute descent reduced to touchdown velocity.
